source_copy_units.headline: A real brief becomes editable PPT proof.
source_copy_units.subhead: Vulca converts a real commercial brief plus selected multimodal tutorial and case data into a code-generated editable PPT deck with trace, QA, and review artifacts.
annotations: brief, source pack, memory, code, and PPT output are shown as one native product path
buyers can judge a repeatable workflow instead of a one-off pretty slide
state_labels: before: prompt-only deck with weak provenance
after: editable PPT generated from selected case data
source_copy_units.speaker_note: Say that the slide is not an image-generation demo; the product output is code-generated editable PPT with traceable inputs.

socket_bindings.placement_rule: bind headline to the primary_carrier and reject detached labels
bind subhead to the support_surface and reject detached labels
bind proof_badges to the proof_object and reject detached labels
bind annotations to the annotation_callout and reject detached labels
bind state_labels to the state_marker and reject detached labels
bind speaker_note to the speaker_note_channel and reject detached labels

text_socket_fusion_contracts.overflow_behavior: move raw detail to viewer and preserve public proof replacement
text_socket_fusion_contracts.surface_terms_policy: public copy must avoid run ids, raw trace names, schema ids, and source brand names

source_copy_units.headline: Sources become design decisions before drawing.
source_copy_units.subhead: The system selects a usecase and allowed source pack, derives product capability memory, then turns it into per-slide message contracts and workflow gates before generation.
annotations: tutorial, case, and multimodal records are reduced into explicit memory and gate objects
the deck can explain why a visual decision was chosen
state_labels: before: raw tutorials sit outside the product
after: source rules are compiled into selectable memory
source_copy_units.speaker_note: Keep the public slide as a pipeline story. Move raw audit detail to the viewer so the slide does not become a compliance checklist.

source_copy_units.headline: The difference is mechanism, not taste.
source_copy_units.subhead: Vulca is differentiated by the case database, skill workflow, bad-control comparison, trace manifest, and code-generated editable PPT output, not by simply producing a nicer-looking slide.
annotations: the prompt-only path lacks source memory, carrier selection, and socket-bound copy
the buyer sees a system boundary rather than a subjective style comparison
state_labels: before: generic deck from a prompt
after: source-bound generated PPT with visible decision route
source_copy_units.speaker_note: Do not render another table. Show the competitor path breaking at the missing data/workflow point, then route to Vulca's editable PPT proof.

source_copy_units.headline: Trace becomes inspectable product proof.
source_copy_units.subhead: Each full-arm slide must carry capability ids, message contract id, workflow gate id, competitor boundary ids, content specificity status, and required product terms in trace before native PPT drawing.
annotations: public proof objects point to trace-only records that remain inspectable in the viewer
reviewers can audit the pipeline without turning the slide into a compliance report
state_labels: before: raw trace text competes with the main message
after: public proof object links to detailed trace
source_copy_units.speaker_note: Show enough proof for trust, but keep raw field enumeration in the viewer where reviewers can inspect it.

source_copy_units.headline: One generated result owns the product moment.
source_copy_units.subhead: The strongest moment is a real brief becoming an editable PPT system: data, memory, workflow gates, code modules, preview, trace, and QA all shown as one product operating loop.
annotations: the source memory and code path converge into one editable native PPT preview
the product feels like an operating system for presentation generation
state_labels: before: separate proof tags and disconnected diagrams
after: one generated result object with attached proof
source_copy_units.speaker_note: This slide should be the least report-like: one product loop, one remembered claim, no raw trace checklist on the public surface.

source_copy_units.headline: The next proof is generated, not claimed.
source_copy_units.subhead: Run 2.57 is a content-layer repair only; Run 2.58 must generate a new four-arm PPT that consumes the content contracts, then pass visual review, native render inspection, source-boundary review, and human approval.
annotations: the narrative proof dataset becomes a required consumer contract for the next PPT generator
the demo stays credible by showing the remaining gate instead of overclaiming
state_labels: before: public release claimed from a thin proof
after: next proof waits for generated comparison and review
source_copy_units.speaker_note: Close calmly: the next action is a generated rerun that consumes the compiler, not a claim of public readiness.
